_notes: TypeScript-only entries from the original (TS2307, TS2339, IMPORT_TYPE_KEYWORD, OVERRIDE_VISIBILITY) are dropped. game-creator templates ship as ES modules in plain JS — these signatures are not applicable.

entries.rootCause: Object accessed before initialization. Common in Phaser create() where an object is used before being constructed, or in Three.js render() where a mesh is referenced before it's added to the scene.

fix.description: Reorder creation statements so that the object is initialized before it is accessed. In Phaser scenes: move construction above first usage in create(). In Three.js: ensure objects are added to scene before render loop touches them.

entries.rootCause: Phaser texture key used in code does not match a key registered in asset-pack.json or loaded via this.load.image/spritesheet. Often a spelling difference or missing frame number suffix.

fix.description: Compare the texture key in code with public/assets/asset-pack.json (or Phaser .load calls in the boot/preload scene). Use the exact key from the asset registry.

entries.rootCause: Phaser animation key referenced via .play() does not exist in animations.json or was never registered via this.anims.create. Key may have been renamed or never created.

fix.description: Cross-reference with public/assets/animations.json or wherever this.anims.create is called. Confirm the animation key exists before play().

entries.rootCause: Scene class not registered in Phaser game config. scene.start() or scene.launch() targets a key that was never added via the GameConfig.scene[] array or game.scene.add().

fix.description: Import the scene class in main.js or core/GameConfig.js and add it to the Phaser game config's scene[] array.

entries.rootCause: Infinite recursion. Common patterns: a method calling itself without a base case, circular scene transitions (Scene A starts B starts A), or an event handler that emits the event it listens for.

fix.description: Trace the call stack to identify the recursive loop. Add a termination condition or break the circular dependency in scene/event flow.

signature.messagePattern: Asset key '(.+)' used in code but not found in asset-pack.json

entries.rootCause: Code references texture/audio keys via this.add.image/sprite/audio or this.load.* that do not exist in the asset registry. Caught by scanning code for string literals and cross-referencing with public/assets/asset-pack.json.

fix.description: Either add the missing key to asset-pack.json or correct the key in source. The check is no-op when asset-pack.json is absent, which is correct for shape-only games.

signature.messagePattern: Scene key '(.+)' used in scene\.start\(\) but not registered

entries.rootCause: scene.start() or scene.launch() targets a scene key that is not in the Phaser game config's scene[] array nor added via game.scene.add(). Will fail at runtime with 'Scene not found'.

fix.description: Register the missing scene in src/main.js or src/core/GameConfig.js. Greppable: every key used in scene.start/launch must appear in either the GameConfig.scene array or a scene.add() call.

signature.messagePattern: Animation key '(.+)' referenced in code but not defined

entries.rootCause: Code plays an animation by key that doesn't exist in animations.json or anims.create. Caught by scanning .play() string-literal arguments and cross-referencing.

fix.description: Verify the chain: spritesheet frames exist in asset-pack.json → animation defined in animations.json or anims.create() → code uses the exact key. No-op when animations.json is absent.
